title: Ambienttangential
summary: Formalizes equality modulo the defining function Q and the condition that a weighted ambient operator descends to boundary data.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Formalizes equality modulo the defining function Q and the condition that a weighted ambient operator descends to boundary data.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/Ambienttangential.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Two ambient functions have the same boundary value if their difference is divisible by Q. This is a semantic alias for EqModQ. In explicit terms, for f, g of type function conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.Eq Mod Q f g.
correspondence.leanType: def SameBoundaryValue (f g : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Weighted boundary equivalence for homogeneous ambient extensions. Besides asking that f - g be a Q-multiple, this records that the error term has the expected shifted homogeneity. This avoids any global cancellation or division by Q. In explicit terms, for w in the real numbers, and f, g of type function conf, its defining expression is there exists h : Function Conf, the function sending U to f U minus g U equals Cal Conf.Qmul h and Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w minus 2 h.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{SameBoundaryValueAtWeight}(w,f,g)=\exists h \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}, (U \longmapsto f U - g U) = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{Qmul} h \land \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} (w - 2) h
correspondence.leanType: def SameBoundaryValueAtWeight (w : ℝ) (f g : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f g if and only if Cal Conf.Eq Mod Q f g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_def (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g ↔ CalConf.EqModQ f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For w in the real numbers, f, g of type Function Conf, and hfg of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value At Weight w f g, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValueAtWeight.sameBoundaryValue {w : ℝ} {f g : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValueAtWeight w f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For h of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.Vanishes Mod Q Cal Conf.Qmul h.
correspondence.leanType: theorem VanishesModQ_Qmul (h : Function Conf) : CalConf.VanishesModQ (CalConf.Qmul h)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Cal Conf.Vanishes Mod Q zero Conf.
correspondence.leanType: theorem VanishesModQ_zero : CalConf.VanishesModQ (zero Conf)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.Eq Mod Q f g if and only if there exists h : Function Conf, the function sending U to f U minus g U equals Cal Conf.Qmul h.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{EqModQ} f g \Longleftrightarrow \exists h \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}, (U \longmapsto f U - g U) = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{Qmul} h
correspondence.leanType: theorem EqModQ_iff_exists_Qmul (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.EqModQ f g ↔ ∃ h : Function Conf, (fun U => f U - g U) = CalConf.Qmul h

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f of type Function Conf, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_refl (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, and hfg of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f g, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value g f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_symm {f g : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g, k of type Function Conf, hfg of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f g, and hgk of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value g k, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f k.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,f,g,k\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;hfg\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} f g,\;hgk\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} g k,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} f k
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_trans {f g k : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g) (hgk : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g k) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f, g of type Function Conf, and h of type f equals g, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f g.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_of_eq {f g : Function Conf} (h : f = g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f₁, f₂, g₁, g₂ of type Function Conf, hf of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f₁ f₂, and hg of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value g₁ g₂, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value add Conf f₁ g₁ add Conf f₂ g₂.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,f₁,f₂,g₁,g₂\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;hf\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} f₁ f₂,\;hg\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} g₁ g₂,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (\operatorname{add} \operatorname{Conf} f₁ g₁) (\operatorname{add} \operatorname{Conf} f₂ g₂)
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_add {f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f₁ f₂) (hg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g₁ g₂) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (add Conf f₁ g₁) (add Conf f₂ g₂)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For f₁, f₂, g₁, g₂ of type Function Conf, hf of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f₁ f₂, and hg of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value g₁ g₂, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value sub Conf f₁ g₁ sub Conf f₂ g₂.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,f₁,f₂,g₁,g₂\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;hf\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} f₁ f₂,\;hg\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} g₁ g₂,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (\operatorname{sub} \operatorname{Conf} f₁ g₁) (\operatorname{sub} \operatorname{Conf} f₂ g₂)
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_sub {f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f₁ f₂) (hg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue g₁ g₂) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (sub Conf f₁ g₁) (sub Conf f₂ g₂)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For c in the real numbers, f, g of type Function Conf, and hfg of type Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f g, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value smul Conf c f smul Conf c g.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,c\colon \mathbb{R},\;f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;hfg\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} f g,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (\operatorname{smul} \operatorname{Conf} c f) (\operatorname{smul} \operatorname{Conf} c g)
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_smul (c : ℝ) {f g : Function Conf} (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (smul Conf c f) (smul Conf c g)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A formal ambient scalar operator. In explicit terms, its defining expression is Function Conf maps to Function Conf.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev Operator : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An ambient operator is tangential if it preserves the equivalence relation given by equality modulo Q. In explicit terms, for p of type operator conf : equals conf, its defining expression is for every f g : Function Conf, Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value f g maps to Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value P f P g.
correspondence.leanType: def IsTangentialOperator (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A weaker-looking but useful formulation: an operator preserves functions vanishing modulo Q. For a general nonlinear operator this is not equivalent to tangentiality. The equivalence requires additivity/subtractivity assumptions. In explicit terms, for p of type operator conf : equals conf, its defining expression is for every f : Function Conf, Cal Conf.Vanishes Mod Q f maps to Cal Conf.Vanishes Mod Q P f.
correspondence.leanType: def PreservesVanishesModQ (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Cal Conf.Is Tangential Operator fun f : Function Conf maps to f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsTangentialOperator.id : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator (fun f : Function Conf => f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For P, R of type Operator Conf : equals Conf, hP of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential Operator P, and hR of type Cal Conf.Is Tangential Operator R, Cal Conf.Is Tangential Operator the function sending f to P R f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsTangentialOperator.comp {P R : Operator (Conf := Conf)} (hP : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator P) (hR : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator R) : CalConf.IsTangentialOperator (fun f => P (R f))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Tangentiality restricted to homogeneous functions of input weight w. This is the right notion for ambient GJMS operators, because powers of the ambient Laplacian are tangential only at the critical input weights. In explicit terms, for p of type operator conf : equals conf, and w in the real numbers, its defining expression is for every f g : Function Conf, Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w f maps to Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w g maps to Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value At Weight w f g maps to Cal Conf.Same Boundary Value P f P g.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeight}(P,w)=\forall {f g \colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf}}, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} w f \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} w g \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValueAtWeight} w f g \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (P f) (P g)
correspondence.leanType: def IsTangentialAtWeight (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) (w : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An operator maps homogeneous functions of weight w₁ to homogeneous functions of weight w₂. In explicit terms, for p of type operator conf : equals conf, and w₁, w₂ in the real numbers, its defining expression is for every f : Function Conf, Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w₁ f maps to Cal Conf.Is XHomogeneous w₂ P f.
correspondence.leanType: def MapsHomogeneousWeight (P : Operator (Conf := Conf)) (w₁ w₂ : ℝ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A weighted tangential ambient operator. This is the structure which should eventually model an operator E[w₁] → E[w₂] defined by choosing an ambient extension, applying an ambient operator, and restricting back to the boundary. Its mathematical data are: input Weight: the real numbers; output Weight: the real numbers; to Operator: Operator Conf : equals Conf; maps Weight: Cal Conf.Maps Homogeneous Weight to Operator input weight output weight; tangential: Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weight to Operator input weight.
correspondence.leanType: structure WeightedTangentialOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The critical input weight for the k-th GJMS-type construction. In explicit terms, for k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is k minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: def criticalWeight (k : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The output weight after applying Δ̃^k at the critical input weight. In explicit terms, for k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is - k minus the dimension n of the ambient calculus divided by 2.
correspondence.leanType: def gjmsOutputWeight (k : ℕ) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Weight bookkeeping for powers of the ambient Laplacian at the GJMS critical weight. This uses the already proved theorem lapPow_isXHomogeneous from Ambientoperator.lean. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_has_GJMS_weight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (CalConf.criticalWeight k) f) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (CalConf.gjmsOutputWeight k) (CalConf.lapPow k f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The formal ambient k-th GJMS candidate, assuming tangentiality has been proved separately. At this abstraction level, the missing nontrivial theorem is exactly the tangentiality of Δ̃^k at the critical weight. In explicit terms, for h of type cal conf.algebraic identities, k in the natural numbers, and htangential of type cal conf.is tangential at weight fun f : function conf maps to cal conf.power of the ambient laplacian k f cal conf.critical weight k, its defining expression is input weight : equals Cal Conf.critical Weight k output weight : equals Cal Conf.gjms Output Weight k to Operator : equals the function sending f to Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian k f maps Weight : equals by intro f hf exact Cal Conf.lap Pow has GJMS weight H k hf tangential : equals htangential.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{ambientGJMSCandidate}(H,k,htangential)=w_{\mathrm{in}} = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{criticalWeight} k w_{\mathrm{out}} = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{gjmsOutputWeight} k \operatorname{toOperator} = f \longmapsto \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k f \operatorname{mapsWeight} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} f \operatorname{hf} \operatorname{exact} \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow\_has\_GJMS\_weight} H k \operatorname{hf} \operatorname{tangential} = \operatorname{htangential}
correspondence.leanType: def ambientGJMSCandidate (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (htangential : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (fun f : Function Conf => CalConf.lapPow k f) (CalConf.criticalWeight k)) : CalConf.WeightedTangentialOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and k in the natural numbers, Cal Conf.Is Tangential At Weight fun f : Function Conf maps to Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian k f Cal Conf.critical Weight k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_isTangentialAtCriticalWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (fun f : Function Conf => CalConf.lapPow k f) (CalConf.criticalWeight k)

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
